page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 144 physical cores and 288 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 576 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Cache specifications include 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and a large 504 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

In benchmark testing, the c4-highcpu-288 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench software compilation. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and bfloat16 operations, which contributes to its top-tier performance in cryptographic and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier status in both cached and uncached read and write operations.

This instance is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as large-scale software compilation, high-performance computing simulations, database operations, and static web serving. While it exhibits strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, its text generation speeds for smaller models are relatively weak, representing a potential tradeoff for real-time inference tasks. Given its high compute density and lack of bundled local storage, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require massive parallel processing power without local disk requirements.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 288 vCPUs and 576 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 144 physical cores and a 2.3 GHz clock speed, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, making it highly effective for cryptographic tasks, physics simulations, and software compilation. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, presenting a resource profile tailored specifically for high-density compute workloads. While it excels in multi-threaded benchmarks and database operations, text generation for smaller language models is a relative bottleneck. It is best suited for high-performance computing, large-scale web serving, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized server featuring 288 vCPUs, 576 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-288 is a compute-optimized server with 288 vCPUs and 576 GB RAM, designed for high-performance parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring two hundred and eighty-eight virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 144 physical cores and 288 vCPUs
Equipped with 576 GB of dedicated RAM and a large 504 MB L3 cache
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix math
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations
Optimized for high-performance computing, software compilation, and static web serving
